F.formula: F = rho_Pearson(attr, |Delta_y(u)|)  =  Statistics.cor(attr, oracle_abs_delta)  (0 if either has zero variance; negatives preserved)
F.plain: Raw Pearson correlation between the method's per-cause attribution magnitudes and the oracle's true absolute causal effects |Delta_y(u)|, scored separately for the content and position output regimes.
F.note: counterfactual.jl: cf_attr = single-cell on-distribution counterfactual per cause (lines 295-306), pr = pearson(cf_attr, odelta) (line 305); triad_extra_dict(f.pearson, f.cf_attr_per_cause, f.oracle_abs_delta) line 713. Record: out/counterfactual_air_raid_content.json (metric_name=pearson_corr_with_oracle, value=0.79). Scorer pilot_ig_vs_oracle.jl pearson() lines 173-178 (raw Statistics.cor, zero-variance->0); F = pearson(attr, oracle_abs_delta) where oracle_abs_delta = abs.(cmap.delta) (true |Delta_y| per cause). Also reports spearman, precision@k, deletion/insertion AUC (paper's auxiliary F metrics). Per-regime split: separate *_content.json and *_position.json records, each carrying its own extra.triad.{F,S,M}.

S.formula: S = (#held-out do(u) with |y_intact + Delta_y_pred - (y_intact + Delta_y_true)| <= epsilon) / |held-out|,  in [0,1]
S.plain: Fit Delta_y = a*attr + b on a calibration half of the do(u) causes, predict the disjoint held-out half, and report the fraction of held-out causes whose predicted output lands within an epsilon band of the oracle's bit-exact re-run.
S.note: common/triad_sm.jl sufficiency_score() lines 111-155 (interleaved calib/held split by seed, least-squares 1-D fit Delta_y~a*attr+b on calib, epsilon = max(0.5, 0.10*heldout_spread)); assembled in triad_extra_dict() lines 173-191.  The new §3 (sec:triad) admits the fraction-within-tolerance [0,1] special case of the held-out predictive test, which this held-out do(u) fit-then-predict estimator satisfies, so it matches. (Detail: epsilon is a self-scaled band 0.10*spread; the [-1,1] correlation form is available but this method reports the [0,1] hit fraction.)

M.plain: Ratio of the number of oracle causal movers (causes with nonzero true Delta_y) to the number of cells the method names above 1e-6 of its own max attribution; null when the oracle finds no mover or the method names nothing.
M.note: common/triad_sm.jl minimality_score() lines 57-86 with name_frac=1e-6, mover_floor=0.0; the above-threshold named set is used (topk kept but not passed), see triad_extra_dict() lines 178-181.  This is the paper's M = |U*|/|U_hat| in (0,1] (standardized everywhere), so it matches the new §3. (Detail: |U*| is taken as all oracle movers with Delta_y>0, an upper bound on the strictly-smallest reproducing subset, so M can be optimistic.)

M.plain: Ratio of the number of oracle causal movers (causes with nonzero true Delta_y) to the number of cells the method names above 1e-6 of its own max attribution; null when the oracle finds no mover or the method names nothing.
M.note: common/triad_sm.jl minimality_score() lines 57-86 with name_frac=1e-6, mover_floor=0.0; the above-threshold named set is used (topk kept but not passed), see triad_extra_dict() lines 178-181. RISE names a dense mask, so |U_hat| >> |U*| and M is small (e.g. 2/26=0.077 in the air_raid content record) -- the intended discrimination of the above-threshold reading. This is the paper's M = |U*|/|U_hat| in (0,1] (standardized everywhere), so it matches the new §3. (Detail: |U*| is taken as all oracle movers with Delta_y>0, an upper bound on the strictly-smallest reproducing subset, so M can be optimistic.)

F.formula: F = rho_Pearson(attr, |Delta_y(u)|)  =  Statistics.cor(attr, oracle_abs_delta)  (0 if either has zero variance; negatives preserved)
F.plain: Raw Pearson correlation between the method's per-cause attribution magnitudes and the oracle's true absolute causal effects |Delta_y(u)|, scored separately for the content and position output regimes.
F.note: saliency.jl: sal_attr = abs.(gradient) per cause (line 338, 426), pr = pearson(sal_attr, odelta) (line 429); triad_extra_dict(f.pearson, f.sal_attr, f.oracle_abs_delta) line 697. Record: out/saliency_air_raid_content.json (value=0.79); position record out/saliency_air_raid_position.json has F=-0.144 (negative preserved). Scorer pilot_ig_vs_oracle.jl pearson() lines 173-178 (raw Statistics.cor, zero-variance->0); F = pearson(attr, oracle_abs_delta) where oracle_abs_delta = abs.(cmap.delta) (true |Delta_y| per cause). Also reports spearman, precision@k, deletion/insertion AUC (paper's auxiliary F metrics). Per-regime split: separate *_content.json and *_position.json records, each carrying its own extra.triad.{F,S,M}.

S.formula: S = (#held-out do(u) with |y_intact + Delta_y_pred - (y_intact + Delta_y_true)| <= epsilon) / |held-out|,  in [0,1]
S.plain: Fit Delta_y = a*attr + b on a calibration half of the do(u) causes, predict the disjoint held-out half, and report the fraction of held-out causes whose predicted output lands within an epsilon band of the oracle's bit-exact re-run.
